frameworks.name: entrainment / Arnold tongue
frameworks.one_line: External oscillatory drive locks endogenous rhythm within a frequency-amplitude wedge.

frameworks.name: thalamo-cortical computational model
frameworks.one_line: Spiking network of cortex and higher-order thalamus reproduces tACS effects.

core_question: How does tACS interact with neuronal spiking to entrain endogenous alpha oscillations in the thalamo-cortical system?
key_assumptions: alpha oscillations arise from thalamo-cortical interactions
weak electric fields (<0.5 mV/mm) can bias spike timing
Arnold tongue framework applies to in vivo entrainment
manipulations: applied alpha-band tACS at human-comparable field strength
varied stimulation parameters mapping the Arnold tongue
compared awake ferret recordings with thalamo-cortical model
key_findings: posterior parietal alpha drives V1 and higher-order visual thalamus
spike-field coherence peaks in the alpha band
fast-spiking inhibitory interneurons couple most strongly to endogenous alpha
tACS entrains alpha following the predicted Arnold tongue, with stronger entrainment of fast-spiking interneurons
interpretations: tACS entrains alpha by preferential phase synchronization of fast-spiking interneurons
stronger endogenous coupling of FS interneurons to alpha explains their preferential response to weak fields
